heading: Periods of limitation on suits

children.heading: Suits by taxpayers for refund
children.label: a Suits by taxpayers for refund

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: No suit or proceeding under section 7422(a) for the recovery of any internal revenue tax, penalty, or other sum, shall be begun before the expiration of 6 months from the date of filing the claim required under such section unless the Secretary renders a decision thereon within that time, nor after the expiration of 2 years from the date of mailing by certified mail or registered mail by the Secretary to the taxpayer of a notice of the disallowance of the part of the claim to which the suit or proceeding relates.

children.heading: Extension of time
children.label: 2 Extension of time
children.text: The 2-year period prescribed in paragraph (1) shall be extended for such period as may be agreed upon in writing between the taxpayer and the Secretary.

children.heading: Waiver of notice of disallowance
children.label: 3 Waiver of notice of disallowance
children.text: If any person files a written waiver of the requirement that he be mailed a notice of disallowance, the 2-year period prescribed in paragraph (1) shall begin on the date such waiver is filed.

children.heading: Reconsideration after mailing of notice
children.label: 4 Reconsideration after mailing of notice
children.text: Any consideration, reconsideration, or action by the Secretary with respect to such claim following the mailing of a notice by certified mail or registered mail of disallowance shall not operate to extend the period within which suit may be begun.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: 5 Cross reference
children.text: For substitution of 120-day period for the 6-month period contained in paragraph (1) in a title 11 case, see of the United States Code.section 505(a)(2) of title 11

children.heading: Suits by United States for recovery of erroneous refunds
children.label: b Suits by United States for recovery of erroneous refunds
children.text: Recovery of an erroneous refund by suit under section 7405 shall be allowed only if such suit is begun within 2 years after the making of such refund, except that such suit may be brought at any time within 5 years from the making of the refund if it appears that any part of the refund was induced by fraud or misrepresentation of a material fact.

children.heading: Suits by persons other than taxpayers
children.label: c Suits by persons other than taxpayers

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Except as provided by paragraph (2), no suit or proceeding under section 7426 shall be begun after the expiration of 2 years from the date of the levy or agreement giving rise to such action.

children.heading: Period when claim is filed
children.label: 2 Period when claim is filed
children.text: If a request is made for the return of property described in section 6343(b), the 2-year period prescribed in paragraph (1) shall be extended for a period of 12 months from the date of filing of such request or for a period of 6 months from the date of mailing by registered or certified mail by the Secretary to the person making such request of a notice of disallowance of the part of the request to which the action relates, whichever is shorter.
